name: Caramel Crunch Pie
recipeIngredient: Remaining sour cream for topping (1/4 cup)
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the crust: Preheat the oven to 350°F. Combine graham cracker crumbs, finely chopped peanuts, and melted butter in a medium bowl. Press into the bottom and halfway up the sides of a 9-inch springform pan.
2. Prepare the filling: Beat cream cheese until smooth. Beat in brown sugar, corn syrup, and cornstarch. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Stir in sour cream and vanilla extract. Stir in chopped candy bars and peanuts, then pour over the crust.
3. Bake the pie: Place the pan in the preheated oven and bake for 15 minutes. Reduce the oven temperature to 300°F and continue baking for an additional 1-1/4 hours, or until the top of the cheesecake is no longer wet or glossy. Remove from the oven and let it cool completely on a wire rack.
4. Prepare the topping: Melt milk chocolate chips in a small saucepan over low heat. Stir in remaining sour cream and brown sugar. Spread over the cooled cheesecake and sprinkle with whole peanuts. Melt caramel candies in a heavy saucepan over low heat, remove from heat, and stir in additional sour cream. Drizzle over the cheesecake.
5. Chill the pie: Cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours before serving.
Makes 16 servings.
